label: general-purpose CPU
note: runs any op-class in software — no accelerator op-graph to fall outside of; the honest floor: everything fits, everything is slow. The baseline a fixed-function NPU must beat to be worth the integration cost.

instances.name: rpi5_cortex_a76
instances.label: Raspberry Pi 5 (Cortex-A76 x4 @ 2.4 GHz)
instances.ext_memory: system LPDDR4X-4267 (shared; 1/2/4/8/16 GiB options, 8 GiB shown)
instances.sources: S15 (Cortex-A76 @2.4GHz x4, no vendor TOPS spec), S16 (LPDDR4X); compute rates DERIVED (see note)
instances.note: no NPU; peaks DERIVED from the Cortex-A76 ISA (2x 128-bit NEON SIMD/core): fp32 = 2.4GHz x 4 cores x 2 pipes x 8 FLOP/128-bit-FMA = 0.15 TFLOPS; int8 = same x 32 ops/128-bit-SDOT = 0.61 TOPS. THEORETICAL arithmetic ceilings — a general CPU on dense conv is memory-bound and sustains a small fraction, so treat the CPU latency band as an upper bound on FPS, not an estimate.
